---
title: Meta-Analysis
description: "Synthesising evidence across studies: fixed- and random-effects models, heterogeneity, publication bias, and network meta-analysis"
---

Meta-analysis combines results from multiple primary studies into a single summary, with an uncertainty that reflects both within-study sampling variation and between-study differences. Done well, it is the highest tier of quantitative evidence; done carelessly, it averages noise with bias and obscures the conclusion it claims to support.

This area covers the statistical machinery of meta-analysis from the two-study case through to network meta-analysis of dozens of interventions, with attention to the PRISMA reporting standards that govern how the review must be documented.

## Topics

- Effect sizes for continuous outcomes: mean difference, standardised mean difference (Hedges' g, Cohen's d)
- Effect sizes for binary outcomes: risk ratio, odds ratio, risk difference
- Effect sizes for correlations and for diagnostic accuracy
- Fixed-effect and random-effects models; inverse-variance weighting
- Heterogeneity: Q statistic, I-squared, tau-squared, prediction intervals
- Subgroup analysis and meta-regression
- Forest plots, funnel plots, and their interpretation
- Publication bias: Egger's test, Begg's test, trim-and-fill, selection models
- Small-study effects and the Hartung-Knapp adjustment for random-effects confidence intervals
- Risk-of-bias assessment (RoB 2, ROBINS-I) and its incorporation in sensitivity analyses
- Individual participant data (IPD) meta-analysis
- Network meta-analysis (NMA): consistency models, inconsistency tests, league tables, SUCRA
- Diagnostic test accuracy meta-analysis: bivariate model, HSROC

Packages: `meta`, `metafor`, `dmetar`, `netmeta`, `gemtc`, `mada`, `robvis`.

<!-- card-grid-injected -->

## Tutorials

::: {.card-grid}

::: {.card}
::: {.kicker}
TUTORIAL
:::
### [Bivariate SROC Curves](bivariate-sroc.qmd)
Hierarchical summary ROC modelling for diagnostic-accuracy meta-analysis
:::

::: {.card}
::: {.kicker}
TUTORIAL
:::
### [Converting Between Effect Sizes](effect-size-conversion.qmd)
Moving between standardised mean differences, correlations, and odds ratios
:::

::: {.card}
::: {.kicker}
TUTORIAL
:::
### [Cumulative Meta-Analysis](cumulative-meta-analysis.qmd)
Sequentially pooling studies in the order they were published
:::

::: {.card}
::: {.kicker}
TUTORIAL
:::
### [Egger's Test for Funnel Asymmetry](egger-test.qmd)
Formal regression test for small-study effects and publication bias
:::

::: {.card}
::: {.kicker}
TUTORIAL
:::
### [Fixed-Effect vs. Random-Effects Meta-Analysis](fixed-vs-random-effects.qmd)
Two ways to pool study-level effect sizes, and the consequences of each for inference and interpretation
:::

::: {.card}
::: {.kicker}
TUTORIAL
:::
### [Forest Plots in Meta-Analysis](forest-plot-meta.qmd)
Standard visualisation of per-study effects, weights, and the pooled estimate
:::

::: {.card}
::: {.kicker}
TUTORIAL
:::
### [Funnel Plots](funnel-plot-meta.qmd)
Visual inspection for small-study effects and publication bias
:::

::: {.card}
::: {.kicker}
TUTORIAL
:::
### [Individual Participant Data Meta-Analysis](ipd-meta-analysis.qmd)
Two-stage vs one-stage analysis of raw data pooled across studies
:::

::: {.card}
::: {.kicker}
TUTORIAL
:::
### [Leave-One-Out Meta-Analysis](leave-one-out.qmd)
Sensitivity of the pooled estimate to each individual study
:::

::: {.card}
::: {.kicker}
TUTORIAL
:::
### [Meta-Analysis of Diagnostic Accuracy](diagnostic-test-meta.qmd)
Bivariate and HSROC models for pooling diagnostic test performance
:::

::: {.card}
::: {.kicker}
TUTORIAL
:::
### [Meta-Regression](meta-regression.qmd)
Explaining between-study heterogeneity with study-level covariates
:::

::: {.card}
::: {.kicker}
TUTORIAL
:::
### [NMA Consistency and Inconsistency](nma-consistency.qmd)
Loop-specific and global tests for direct-indirect evidence agreement
:::

::: {.card}
::: {.kicker}
TUTORIAL
:::
### [NMA League Tables](nma-league-tables.qmd)
Pairwise treatment contrasts in a compact triangular grid
:::

::: {.card}
::: {.kicker}
TUTORIAL
:::
### [Network Meta-Analysis: Introduction](network-meta-analysis-intro.qmd)
Combining direct and indirect comparisons across multiple treatments
:::

::: {.card}
::: {.kicker}
TUTORIAL
:::
### [Pooling Correlation Coefficients](correlation-pooling.qmd)
Fisher z transformation for meta-analysis of correlations
:::

::: {.card}
::: {.kicker}
TUTORIAL
:::
### [Pooling Log-Odds Ratios](log-odds-ratio-pooling.qmd)
Fixed and random-effects meta-analysis of binary outcomes on the log scale
:::

::: {.card}
::: {.kicker}
TUTORIAL
:::
### [Pooling Risk Ratios](risk-ratio-pooling.qmd)
Meta-analysis of binary outcomes on the risk-ratio scale
:::

::: {.card}
::: {.kicker}
TUTORIAL
:::
### [Prediction Intervals in Meta-Analysis](prediction-interval-meta.qmd)
The plausible range of a future study's true effect under random-effects
:::

::: {.card}
::: {.kicker}
TUTORIAL
:::
### [Quantifying Heterogeneity: Q and I^2](heterogeneity-q-i2.qmd)
Cochran's Q, I-squared, and their interpretation in meta-analysis
:::

::: {.card}
::: {.kicker}
TUTORIAL
:::
### [SUCRA Rankings](nma-sucra.qmd)
Surface Under the Cumulative Ranking curve for treatment comparisons
:::

::: {.card}
::: {.kicker}
TUTORIAL
:::
### [Selection Models for Publication Bias](selection-models-bias.qmd)
Weighted-distribution models for adjusting meta-analysis for selection
:::

::: {.card}
::: {.kicker}
TUTORIAL
:::
### [Standardised Mean Difference: Hedges' g](smd-hedges-g.qmd)
Small-sample-corrected standardised effect size for continuous outcomes
:::

::: {.card}
::: {.kicker}
TUTORIAL
:::
### [Subgroup Meta-Analysis](subgroup-meta-analysis.qmd)
Pooling within strata and testing for between-subgroup heterogeneity
:::

::: {.card}
::: {.kicker}
TUTORIAL
:::
### [Tau-Squared Estimation](tau-squared-estimation.qmd)
Estimators of between-study variance: DL, REML, PM, SJ, EB
:::

::: {.card}
::: {.kicker}
TUTORIAL
:::
### [Trim-and-Fill](trim-and-fill.qmd)
Adjusting the pooled estimate for apparent funnel asymmetry
:::

:::
